trans-unit.source: Geodata Management with Sharepoint
trans-unit.target: Geodata Management with Sharepoint

trans-unit.source: Small organisations often struggle to keep geospatial data consistent and accessible. A simple, well-structured geodata hub helps **standardise GIS work**, **prepare for responses**, and **onboard new staff** efficiently. This guide outlines a practical approach using **SharePoint**, which many organisations already use for document management.
trans-unit.target: Small organisations often struggle to keep geospatial data consistent and accessible. A simple, well-structured geodata hub helps **standardise GIS work**, **prepare for responses**, and **onboard new staff** efficiently. This guide outlines a practical approach using **SharePoint**, which many organisations already use for document management.

trans-unit.source: Purpose & Audience
trans-unit.target: Purpose & Audience

trans-unit.source: **Who**: Small organisations starting or formalising GIS work.
trans-unit.target: **Who**: Small organisations starting or formalising GIS work.

trans-unit.source: **Why**: Establish a lightweight, standardised structure for **data, projects, and templates**.
trans-unit.target: **Why**: Establish a lightweight, standardised structure for **data, projects, and templates**.

trans-unit.source: **Outcome**: Faster onboarding, fewer broken links, consistent maps and analyses.
trans-unit.target: **Outcome**: Faster onboarding, fewer broken links, consistent maps and analyses.

trans-unit.source: The role of the Data Steward
trans-unit.target: The role of the Data Steward

trans-unit.source: A **data steward** is a designated person (or small team) responsible for:
trans-unit.target: A **data steward** is a designated person (or small team) responsible for:

trans-unit.source: Approving and uploading new datasets into `10_Data` and `20_Raster_Data/`.
trans-unit.target: Approving and uploading new datasets into `10_Data` and `20_Raster_Data/`.

trans-unit.source: Managing permissions (e.g. who can edit vs. who can only view).
trans-unit.target: Managing permissions (e.g. who can edit vs. who can only view).

trans-unit.source: Supporting colleagues with questions about dataset quality or standards.
trans-unit.target: Supporting colleagues with questions about dataset quality or standards.

trans-unit.source: Having a clear steward role prevents accidental overwrites and ensures that all staff work with **trusted, consistent data**.
trans-unit.target: Having a clear steward role prevents accidental overwrites and ensures that all staff work with **trusted, consistent data**.

trans-unit.source: Example Folder Structures
trans-unit.target: Example Folder Structures

trans-unit.source: Below are three possible structures, from simple to more advanced. Choose the one that matches your organisation’s size and capacity.
trans-unit.target: Below are three possible structures, from simple to more advanced. Choose the one that matches your organisation’s size and capacity.

trans-unit.source: 1. Minimalist Setup (very small teams)
trans-unit.target: 1. Minimalist Setup (very small teams)

trans-unit.source: 💡 **Use when:** The team is very small, with limited data and little overlap between users. This structure is quick to set up and easy for non-technical colleagues.
trans-unit.target: 💡 **Use when:** The team is very small, with limited data and little overlap between users. This structure is quick to set up and easy for non-technical colleagues.

trans-unit.source: ⚠️ **Limitation:** Everything is mixed into one `Data/` folder — it is not obvious which datasets are authoritative vs. drafts, and scaling up later can get messy.
trans-unit.target: ⚠️ **Limitation:** Everything is mixed into one `Data/` folder — it is not obvious which datasets are authoritative vs. drafts, and scaling up later can get messy.

trans-unit.source: **Naming conventions for Minimalist Setup**
trans-unit.target: **Naming conventions for Minimalist Setup**

trans-unit.source: Keep filenames simple and descriptive, e.g.:
trans-unit.target: Keep filenames simple and descriptive, e.g.:

trans-unit.source: For projects, prefix deliverables with project name, e.g.:
trans-unit.target: For projects, prefix deliverables with project name, e.g.:

trans-unit.source: Dates can be optional here, since the dataset volume is low.
trans-unit.target: Dates can be optional here, since the dataset volume is low.

trans-unit.source: 2. Balanced Setup (general small organisations)
trans-unit.target: 2. Balanced Setup (general small organisations)

trans-unit.source: **💡 Use when:** The organisation wants to keep all curated data together in `10_Data/`, while separating raster data (`20_Raster_Data/`), shared styles (`30_Styles_Templates/`), drafts (`40_Working_Data/`), and project outputs (`50_Projects/`). This setup works well for most small NGOs.
trans-unit.target: **💡 Use when:** The organisation wants to keep all curated data together in `10_Data/`, while separating raster data (`20_Raster_Data/`), shared styles (`30_Styles_Templates/`), drafts (`40_Working_Data/`), and project outputs (`50_Projects/`). This setup works well for most small NGOs.

trans-unit.source: **⚠️ Limitation:** Staff need to understand that `10_Data/` and `20_Raster_Data/` are read-only. If edits are saved there, datasets can be overwritten or corrupted. :::{note} Large raster files (e.g., DEMs, satellite scenes, wind or rainfall rasters) can slow down OneDrive sync and fill local drives quickly.
trans-unit.target: **⚠️ Limitation:** Staff need to understand that `10_Data/` and `20_Raster_Data/` are read-only. If edits are saved there, datasets can be overwritten or corrupted. :::{note} Large raster files (e.g., DEMs, satellite scenes, wind or rainfall rasters) can slow down OneDrive sync and fill local drives quickly.

trans-unit.source: To reduce issues:
trans-unit.target: To reduce issues:

trans-unit.source: Keep only the most important raster layers in `20_Raster_Data/`.
trans-unit.target: Keep only the most important raster layers in `20_Raster_Data/`.

trans-unit.source: Archive old raster versions in a separate library if needed. ::: **Naming conventions for Balanced Setup (general use)**
trans-unit.target: Archive old raster versions in a separate library if needed. ::: **Naming conventions for Balanced Setup (general use)**

trans-unit.source: To keep files easy to find and avoid duplication, use a consistent naming pattern:
trans-unit.target: To keep files easy to find and avoid duplication, use a consistent naming pattern:

trans-unit.source: **Data (all shared):** Pattern: `[Theme]_[Location/ADMlevel]_[Source]_[YYYYMM].[ext]` *Example:* `Boundaries_ADM2_MDG_GADM_202407.gpkg`
trans-unit.target: **Data (all shared):** Pattern: `[Theme]_[Location/ADMlevel]_[Source]_[YYYYMM].[ext]` *Example:* `Boundaries_ADM2_MDG_GADM_202407.gpkg`

trans-unit.source: **Raster data:** Pattern: `[Theme]_[Location]_[Source]_[YYYYMM].[ext]` *Example:* `Rainfall_MDG_CHIRPS_202502.tif`
trans-unit.target: **Raster data:** Pattern: `[Theme]_[Location]_[Source]_[YYYYMM].[ext]` *Example:* `Rainfall_MDG_CHIRPS_202502.tif`

trans-unit.source: **Projects:** Pattern: `[ProjectName]_[OutputType]_[Region]_[YYYYMM]_vX.[ext]` *Example:* `HealthAssessment_Report_SOM_202503_v1.pdf`
trans-unit.target: **Projects:** Pattern: `[ProjectName]_[OutputType]_[Region]_[YYYYMM]_vX.[ext]` *Example:* `HealthAssessment_Report_SOM_202503_v1.pdf`

trans-unit.source: 3. Extended Setup (with EAP workflows)
trans-unit.target: 3. Extended Setup (with EAP workflows)

trans-unit.source: 💡 **Use when:** The organisation wants to keep all curated data together in `10_Data/`, while separating **heavy raster data** (`20_Raster_Data/`), **shared styles** (`30_Styles_Templates/`), and **project-specific or EAP workflows**.
trans-unit.target: 💡 **Use when:** The organisation wants to keep all curated data together in `10_Data/`, while separating **heavy raster data** (`20_Raster_Data/`), **shared styles** (`30_Styles_Templates/`), and **project-specific or EAP workflows**.

trans-unit.source: ⚠️ **Limitation:** Staff need to understand that `10_Data/` and `20_Raster_Data/` are **read-only**. If users save edits there, datasets can be overwritten or corrupted.
trans-unit.target: ⚠️ **Limitation:** Staff need to understand that `10_Data/` and `20_Raster_Data/` are **read-only**. If users save edits there, datasets can be overwritten or corrupted.

trans-unit.source: Keep only the most important raster layers in `20_Raster_Data/`.
trans-unit.target: Keep only the most important raster layers in `20_Raster_Data/`.

trans-unit.source: Archive older rasters in `30_Snapshots/` if you need to keep historical versions. :::
trans-unit.target: Archive older rasters in `30_Snapshots/` if you need to keep historical versions. :::

trans-unit.source: **Naming conventions for Balanced Setup (with EAPs)**
trans-unit.target: **Naming conventions for Balanced Setup (with EAPs)**

trans-unit.source: To keep files easy to find and avoid duplicates, use a consistent naming pattern. Below are suggested conventions with examples:
trans-unit.target: To keep files easy to find and avoid duplicates, use a consistent naming pattern. Below are suggested conventions with examples:

trans-unit.source: **Data (all shared):** Pattern: `[Theme]_[Location/ADMlevel]_[Source]_[YYYYMM].[ext]` Example: `Boundaries_ADM2_MDG_GADM_202407.gpkg`
trans-unit.target: **Data (all shared):** Pattern: `[Theme]_[Location/ADMlevel]_[Source]_[YYYYMM].[ext]` Example: `Boundaries_ADM2_MDG_GADM_202407.gpkg`

trans-unit.source: **Raster data:** Pattern: `[Theme]_[Location]_[Source]_[YYYYMM].[ext]` Example: `Rainfall_MDG_CHIRPS_202502.tif`
trans-unit.target: **Raster data:** Pattern: `[Theme]_[Location]_[Source]_[YYYYMM].[ext]` Example: `Rainfall_MDG_CHIRPS_202502.tif`

trans-unit.source: **EAP Models:** Pattern: `Model_[Hazard]_[Version]_[YYYYMM].[ext]` Example: `Model_CycloneTrigger_v3_202502.model3`
trans-unit.target: **EAP Models:** Pattern: `Model_[Hazard]_[Version]_[YYYYMM].[ext]` Example: `Model_CycloneTrigger_v3_202502.model3`

trans-unit.source: **Activations:** Pattern: `Cyclone_[Name]_[Year]_[OutputType]_[Region].[ext]` Example: `Cyclone_Freddy_2023_ImpactMap_ADM2_MDG.pdf`
trans-unit.target: **Activations:** Pattern: `Cyclone_[Name]_[Year]_[OutputType]_[Region].[ext]` Example: `Cyclone_Freddy_2023_ImpactMap_ADM2_MDG.pdf`

trans-unit.source: **Projects:** Pattern: `[ProjectName]_[OutputType]_[Region]_[YYYYMM]_vX.[ext]` Example: `ClimateRiskAssessment_Map_MDG_202502_v1.pdf`
trans-unit.target: **Projects:** Pattern: `[ProjectName]_[OutputType]_[Region]_[YYYYMM]_vX.[ext]` Example: `ClimateRiskAssessment_Map_MDG_202502_v1.pdf`

trans-unit.source: Advantages, Limitations, and Permissions
trans-unit.target: Advantages, Limitations, and Permissions

trans-unit.source: When setting up a geodata hub in SharePoint, there are clear benefits, but also some important boundaries to be aware of. Below is a short overview of the **advantages**, the **limitations**, and how to handle **permissions** effectively.
trans-unit.target: When setting up a geodata hub in SharePoint, there are clear benefits, but also some important boundaries to be aware of. Below is a short overview of the **advantages**, the **limitations**, and how to handle **permissions** effectively.

trans-unit.source: Advantages
trans-unit.target: Advantages

trans-unit.source: Using SharePoint for geodata management gives small organisations a strong starting point:
trans-unit.target: Using SharePoint for geodata management gives small organisations a strong starting point:

trans-unit.source: **Standardisation** — all staff work with the same datasets, style files, and map templates, which reduces confusion and ensures consistent outputs.
trans-unit.target: **Standardisation** — all staff work with the same datasets, style files, and map templates, which reduces confusion and ensures consistent outputs.

trans-unit.source: **Easy onboarding** — new colleagues don’t waste time hunting for files; the folder structure makes it obvious where to look.
trans-unit.target: **Easy onboarding** — new colleagues don’t waste time hunting for files; the folder structure makes it obvious where to look.

trans-unit.source: **Built-in versioning** — SharePoint automatically stores file history, so it’s easy to recover previous versions if mistakes happen.
trans-unit.target: **Built-in versioning** — SharePoint automatically stores file history, so it’s easy to recover previous versions if mistakes happen.

trans-unit.source: **Flexible permissions** — you can decide who can only view, who can edit, and who has full control.
trans-unit.target: **Flexible permissions** — you can decide who can only view, who can edit, and who has full control.

trans-unit.source: **Integration with QGIS** — through OneDrive sync, datasets can be accessed locally and reliably within QGIS projects.
trans-unit.target: **Integration with QGIS** — through OneDrive sync, datasets can be accessed locally and reliably within QGIS projects.

trans-unit.source: Limitations
trans-unit.target: Limitations

trans-unit.source: However, SharePoint is not a GIS database, and this introduces some constraints:
trans-unit.target: However, SharePoint is not a GIS database, and this introduces some constraints:

trans-unit.source: **Concurrency issues** — file-based geodata formats (like GeoPackage) can corrupt if multiple people try to edit them simultaneously.
trans-unit.target: **Concurrency issues** — file-based geodata formats (like GeoPackage) can corrupt if multiple people try to edit them simultaneously.

trans-unit.source: **Performance bottlenecks** — very large rasters or high-frequency edits will quickly become slow; in these cases, a spatial database such as PostGIS is better.
trans-unit.target: **Performance bottlenecks** — very large rasters or high-frequency edits will quickly become slow; in these cases, a spatial database such as PostGIS is better.

trans-unit.source: **Discipline required** — the system relies on users consistently following folder structures and naming conventions; otherwise, the hub loses its value.
trans-unit.target: **Discipline required** — the system relies on users consistently following folder structures and naming conventions; otherwise, the hub loses its value.

trans-unit.source: :::{important} Think of SharePoint as a **distribution and coordination hub**. For **multi-user editing** or **large-scale storage**, you’ll need a database backend. :::
trans-unit.target: :::{important} Think of SharePoint as a **distribution and coordination hub**. For **multi-user editing** or **large-scale storage**, you’ll need a database backend. :::

trans-unit.source: Permissions
trans-unit.target: Permissions

trans-unit.source: Managing access well is essential to protect authoritative data while allowing collaboration. A good practice is to differentiate between **authoritative data**, **working data**, and **project outputs**:
trans-unit.target: Managing access well is essential to protect authoritative data while allowing collaboration. A good practice is to differentiate between **authoritative data**, **working data**, and **project outputs**:

trans-unit.source: **Authoritative_Data** — read-only for most staff; only designated data stewards can upload or edit.
trans-unit.target: **Authoritative_Data** — read-only for most staff; only designated data stewards can upload or edit.

trans-unit.source: **Working_Data** — restricted to editors; enable check-in/check-out so files aren’t overwritten accidentally.
trans-unit.target: **Working_Data** — restricted to editors; enable check-in/check-out so files aren’t overwritten accidentally.

trans-unit.source: **Projects** — editable by project teams, but can be read-only for others to avoid unwanted edits.
trans-unit.target: **Projects** — editable by project teams, but can be read-only for others to avoid unwanted edits.

trans-unit.source: **Sensitive datasets** — if distribution needs to be controlled, use *View-only* links combined with *Block download*.
trans-unit.target: **Sensitive datasets** — if distribution needs to be controlled, use *View-only* links combined with *Block download*.

trans-unit.source: **Keep permissions simple.** Instead of assigning rights to individuals, create SharePoint groups such as:
trans-unit.target: **Keep permissions simple.** Instead of assigning rights to individuals, create SharePoint groups such as:

trans-unit.source: *Owners* (Full Control)
trans-unit.target: *Owners* (Full Control)

trans-unit.source: *Editors* (Edit)
trans-unit.target: *Editors* (Edit)

trans-unit.source: *Viewers* (Read)
trans-unit.target: *Viewers* (Read)

trans-unit.source: This makes management much easier when staff join or leave the organisation.
trans-unit.target: This makes management much easier when staff join or leave the organisation.

trans-unit.source: Metadata & Cataloguing
trans-unit.target: Metadata & Cataloguing

trans-unit.source: Even if your files are well-organised in folders, it can still be hard for new staff to know **which dataset is the latest** or **what each file is used for**. This is where *metadata* comes in. In SharePoint, you can add extra information fields (columns) to your libraries. These fields make it easy to filter, sort, and search across all data — without relying only on file names.
trans-unit.target: Even if your files are well-organised in folders, it can still be hard for new staff to know **which dataset is the latest** or **what each file is used for**. This is where *metadata* comes in. In SharePoint, you can add extra information fields (columns) to your libraries. These fields make it easy to filter, sort, and search across all data — without relying only on file names.

trans-unit.source: Why use metadata?
trans-unit.target: Why use metadata?

trans-unit.source: **Clarity**: Staff can quickly see which dataset is authoritative, draft, or outdated.
trans-unit.target: **Clarity**: Staff can quickly see which dataset is authoritative, draft, or outdated.

trans-unit.source: **Consistency**: Everyone uses the same standard fields.
trans-unit.target: **Consistency**: Everyone uses the same standard fields.

trans-unit.source: **Search & filter**: Instead of browsing dozens of folders, users can filter by country, hazard, or date updated.
trans-unit.target: **Search & filter**: Instead of browsing dozens of folders, users can filter by country, hazard, or date updated.

trans-unit.source: **Documentation**: Key context (like source and update cycle) is stored with the file itself, not just in someone’s memory.
trans-unit.target: **Documentation**: Key context (like source and update cycle) is stored with the file itself, not just in someone’s memory.

trans-unit.source: Suggested metadata fields
trans-unit.target: Suggested metadata fields

trans-unit.source: When creating or editing a SharePoint library, you can add simple columns such as:
trans-unit.target: When creating or editing a SharePoint library, you can add simple columns such as:

trans-unit.source: **Theme** (e.g. Boundaries, Health, Logistics, Hazards)
trans-unit.target: **Theme** (e.g. Boundaries, Health, Logistics, Hazards)

trans-unit.source: **Location / ISO3 code** (e.g. MDG, SOM, region name)
trans-unit.target: **Location / ISO3 code** (e.g. MDG, SOM, region name)

trans-unit.source: **Source** (e.g. WFP, UNOCHA, OSM)
trans-unit.target: **Source** (e.g. WFP, UNOCHA, OSM)

trans-unit.source: **DateUpdated** (YYYYMMDD)
trans-unit.target: **DateUpdated** (YYYYMMDD)

trans-unit.source: **Contact / Owner** (responsible person or team)
trans-unit.target: **Contact / Owner** (responsible person or team)

trans-unit.source: **URL** (link to the original data source or documentation)
trans-unit.target: **URL** (link to the original data source or documentation)

trans-unit.source: :::{tip} You don’t need to tag *everything*. Start with 3–4 core fields that help your team find data quickly. For example: *Theme*, *Location*, *Source*, and *DateUpdated*. More can be added later if needed. :::
trans-unit.target: :::{tip} You don’t need to tag *everything*. Start with 3–4 core fields that help your team find data quickly. For example: *Theme*, *Location*, *Source*, and *DateUpdated*. More can be added later if needed. :::

trans-unit.source: YouTube video player
trans-unit.target: YouTube video player

source.bpt: <iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/qB2acPUSF_o?si=JyjVHEqcTNHqTKUK" [#$sd1_sf29_tu2] frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerpolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowfullscreen>

target.bpt: <iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/qB2acPUSF_o?si=JyjVHEqcTNHqTKUK" [#$sd1_sf29_tu2] frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerpolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowfullscreen>

trans-unit.source: Implementation Guide (Quick Start)
trans-unit.target: Implementation Guide (Quick Start)

trans-unit.source: Setting up a geodata hub in SharePoint is straightforward. Follow these steps to get started:
trans-unit.target: Setting up a geodata hub in SharePoint is straightforward. Follow these steps to get started:

trans-unit.source: **Create a new SharePoint document library**
trans-unit.target: **Create a new SharePoint document library**

trans-unit.source: Name it clearly, e.g., `GIS_Hub`.
trans-unit.target: Name it clearly, e.g., `GIS_Hub`.

trans-unit.source: This will be the root space for all your geodata folders.
trans-unit.target: This will be the root space for all your geodata folders.

trans-unit.source: **Set up the folder structure**
trans-unit.target: **Set up the folder structure**

trans-unit.source: Recreate the agreed structure (Minimalist, Balanced, or Extended) inside the library.
trans-unit.target: Recreate the agreed structure (Minimalist, Balanced, or Extended) inside the library.

trans-unit.source: Example for the Balanced Setup:
trans-unit.target: Example for the Balanced Setup:

trans-unit.source: **Configure permissions**
trans-unit.target: **Configure permissions**

trans-unit.source: Use SharePoint groups: *Owners* (Full Control), *Editors* (Edit), *Viewers* (Read).
trans-unit.target: Use SharePoint groups: *Owners* (Full Control), *Editors* (Edit), *Viewers* (Read).

trans-unit.source: Set `10_Data/` and `20_Raster_Data/` as read-only for most users.
trans-unit.target: Set `10_Data/` and `20_Raster_Data/` as read-only for most users.

trans-unit.source: Restrict `40_Working_Data/` to editors only.
trans-unit.target: Restrict `40_Working_Data/` to editors only.

trans-unit.source: **Add initial content**
trans-unit.target: **Add initial content**

trans-unit.source: Upload authoritative datasets into `10_Data/` (boundaries, roads, exposure layers).
trans-unit.target: Upload authoritative datasets into `10_Data/` (boundaries, roads, exposure layers).

trans-unit.source: Upload a few DEMs or land cover rasters into `20_Raster_Data/`.
trans-unit.target: Upload a few DEMs or land cover rasters into `20_Raster_Data/`.

trans-unit.source: Add shared QGIS styles (`.qml`) and layouts (`.qpt`) into `30_Styles_Templates/`.
trans-unit.target: Add shared QGIS styles (`.qml`) and layouts (`.qpt`) into `30_Styles_Templates/`.

trans-unit.source: **Enable OneDrive sync for offline access**
trans-unit.target: **Enable OneDrive sync for offline access**

trans-unit.source: Staff can sync the `GIS_Hub` library to their laptops.
trans-unit.target: Staff can sync the `GIS_Hub` library to their laptops.

trans-unit.source: This allows QGIS to use local file paths (more stable than online links).
trans-unit.target: This allows QGIS to use local file paths (more stable than online links).

trans-unit.source: **Document SOPs in **`00_Admin/`**
trans-unit.target: **Document SOPs in **`00_Admin/`**

trans-unit.source: Add a simple `ReadMe.md` or `SOPs.pdf` explaining:
trans-unit.target: Add a simple `ReadMe.md` or `SOPs.pdf` explaining:

trans-unit.source: Which folders are read-only.
trans-unit.target: Which folders are read-only.

trans-unit.source: How to name files.
trans-unit.target: How to name files.

trans-unit.source: Where to save drafts vs. final outputs.
trans-unit.target: Where to save drafts vs. final outputs.

trans-unit.source: **Onboard staff**
trans-unit.target: **Onboard staff**

trans-unit.source: Show new staff where to find data, projects, and styles.
trans-unit.target: Show new staff where to find data, projects, and styles.

trans-unit.source: Emphasise that only data stewards update the `10_Data/` and `20_Raster_Data/` folders.
trans-unit.target: Emphasise that only data stewards update the `10_Data/` and `20_Raster_Data/` folders.

trans-unit.source: :::{tip} Start small. Don’t upload everything at once. Begin with the datasets most often used in your projects (e.g., admin boundaries, health facilities, WorldPop). You can expand later as the team gets used to the system. :::
trans-unit.target: :::{tip} Start small. Don’t upload everything at once. Begin with the datasets most often used in your projects (e.g., admin boundaries, health facilities, WorldPop). You can expand later as the team gets used to the system. :::

trans-unit.source: Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs)
trans-unit.target: Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs)

trans-unit.source: A clear folder structure is only useful if everyone applies it consistently. To avoid confusion and data loss, organisations should agree on a few simple rules. These **Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs)** can serve as a starting point:
trans-unit.target: A clear folder structure is only useful if everyone applies it consistently. To avoid confusion and data loss, organisations should agree on a few simple rules. These **Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs)** can serve as a starting point:

trans-unit.source: **Data upload** Only data stewards may add or update files in `10_Data/` and `20_Raster_Data/`. Other staff can download these datasets but not modify them.
trans-unit.target: **Data upload** Only data stewards may add or update files in `10_Data/` and `20_Raster_Data/`. Other staff can download these datasets but not modify them.

trans-unit.source: **File naming** Always follow the agreed naming convention (see section on naming). This ensures datasets are easy to find and avoids duplication.
trans-unit.target: **File naming** Always follow the agreed naming convention (see section on naming). This ensures datasets are easy to find and avoids duplication.

trans-unit.source: **Working data**
trans-unit.target: **Working data**

trans-unit.source: Use `40_Working_Data/` only for drafts, intermediate processing, or temporary edits.
trans-unit.target: Use `40_Working_Data/` only for drafts, intermediate processing, or temporary edits.

trans-unit.source: To avoid sync problems (especially with large files), staff can also keep working files **locally on their own computer**.
trans-unit.target: To avoid sync problems (especially with large files), staff can also keep working files **locally on their own computer**.

trans-unit.source: Before weekends, or whenever results need to be shared with the team (for backup and contingencies), upload the relevant files to `40_Working_Data/`.
trans-unit.target: Before weekends, or whenever results need to be shared with the team (for backup and contingencies), upload the relevant files to `40_Working_Data/`.

trans-unit.source: **Project folders** Each project must have its own subfolder in `50_Projects/` (e.g., `FloodResponse_2025/`). Store there:
trans-unit.target: **Project folders** Each project must have its own subfolder in `50_Projects/` (e.g., `FloodResponse_2025/`). Store there:

trans-unit.source: QGIS projects (`.qgz`)
trans-unit.target: QGIS projects (`.qgz`)

trans-unit.source: Final analysis outputs (maps, reports, tables)
trans-unit.target: Final analysis outputs (maps, reports, tables)

trans-unit.source: A copy of the **datasets actually used in the project** (so the work can be reproduced later).
trans-unit.target: A copy of the **datasets actually used in the project** (so the work can be reproduced later).

trans-unit.source: **Templates** Always apply the shared QGIS styles and layouts from `30_Styles_Templates/` when producing maps or analyses. This keeps outputs consistent across the organisation.
trans-unit.target: **Templates** Always apply the shared QGIS styles and layouts from `30_Styles_Templates/` when producing maps or analyses. This keeps outputs consistent across the organisation.

trans-unit.source: **EAP workflows (if relevant)** For anticipatory action, store baseline data in `50_Cyclone_EAP/Fixed_Data/`, models in `50_Cyclone_EAP/Models/`, and create a new folder for each activation under `50_Cyclone_EAP/Activations/`.
trans-unit.target: **EAP workflows (if relevant)** For anticipatory action, store baseline data in `50_Cyclone_EAP/Fixed_Data/`, models in `50_Cyclone_EAP/Models/`, and create a new folder for each activation under `50_Cyclone_EAP/Activations/`.

trans-unit.source: **Change log** Keep a simple log in `00_Admin/` (e.g., `Change_Log.xlsx`) where stewards record updates to data or templates.
trans-unit.target: **Change log** Keep a simple log in `00_Admin/` (e.g., `Change_Log.xlsx`) where stewards record updates to data or templates.

trans-unit.source: **Permissions** Manage access through SharePoint groups, not individuals:
trans-unit.target: **Permissions** Manage access through SharePoint groups, not individuals:

trans-unit.source: *Owners* → Full Control
trans-unit.target: *Owners* → Full Control

trans-unit.source: *Editors* → Edit (e.g., for `40_Working_Data/` and project folders)
trans-unit.target: *Editors* → Edit (e.g., for `40_Working_Data/` and project folders)

trans-unit.source: *Viewers* → Read-only (for most data folders)
trans-unit.target: *Viewers* → Read-only (for most data folders)

trans-unit.source: :::{tip} Short SOPs are most effective if they fit on **one page**. Keep them simple and review once a year to make sure they are still relevant. Emphasise that **day-to-day work files can stay on individual laptops**. Only upload to `40_Working_Data/` before weekends, handovers, or when results need to be shared for team use and contingency. :::
trans-unit.target: :::{tip} Short SOPs are most effective if they fit on **one page**. Keep them simple and review once a year to make sure they are still relevant. Emphasise that **day-to-day work files can stay on individual laptops**. Only upload to `40_Working_Data/` before weekends, handovers, or when results need to be shared for team use and contingency. :::
